prompt_text: Open source or affordable productivity tools instead of Spotlight
response:
# Open Source & Affordable Productivity Alternatives

## **Search/Launcher (Spotlight Replacements)**
- **[Alfred](https://www.alfredapp.com/)** (Mac, freemium) - More powerful than Spotlight, great workflows
- **[Raycast](https://www.raycast.com/)** (Mac, free tier) - Modern, extensible, snappy
- **[Ueli](https://ueli.app/)** (Windows/Mac, open source) - Lightweight launcher
- **[Cerebro](https://cerebroapp.com/)** (Cross-platform, open source) - Simple & fast

## **Note-Taking**
- **[Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/)** (Free, local-first) - Knowledge base, markdown
- **[Joplin](https://joplinapp.org/)** (Open source, free) - Evernote alternative
- **[Logseq](https://logseq.com/)** (Open source, free) - Knowledge management
- **[Notesnook](https://notesnook.com/)** (Free tier, private) - Encrypted notes

## **Task Management**
- **[Vikunja](https://vikunja.io/)** (Open source, free) - Self-hosted to-do lists
- **[Plane](https://plane.so/)** (Open source) - Notion alternative, lightweight
- **[OpenTasks](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.dmfs.tasks)** (Open source, Android)

## **Office Suite**
- **[LibreOffice](https://www.libreoffice.org/)** (Open source, free) - Documents, sheets, presentations
- **[Collabora Online](https://www.collaboraoffice.com/)** (Self-hosted option)

## **Password Manager**
- **[Bitwarden](https://bitwarden.com/)** (Freemium) - Open source, affordable
- **[KeePass](https://keepass.info/)** (Open source, free) - Local-first

**What's your main use case?** I can suggest more specific tools.
